# VanishDrop

> An end-to-end encrypted one-time file-sharing service. The browser
> generates a 256-bit AES-GCM key, encrypts the file locally, uploads
> only the ciphertext, and embeds the key in the URL fragment so the
> server never sees it.

VanishDrop is a small, auditable demonstration of how to build an
encrypted file-drop tool where the server is honest-but-curious.

## Design in one sentence

A 32-byte AES-GCM key + 12-byte IV are generated client-side, the file
is encrypted with the Web Crypto API, only the ciphertext + IV + name
+ TTL is POSTed to the server, and the key travels in the URL fragment
(`#k=…`) which browsers never send in HTTP.

## Documentation

- [README.md](README.md): Quickstart, API surface, trust model.
- [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md): Full threat model and intentional non-goals.
- [ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md): Module-by-module reference.
- [.repobility/access.yml](.repobility/access.yml): Endpoint-by-endpoint
  authorization matrix.
- [docs/adr/](docs/adr/): Architecture Decision Records.

## Source files

- `app/main.py`: FastAPI app — upload, peek, atomic download, SPA shell.
- `frontend/src/crypto.ts`: Web Crypto API wrapper — AES-GCM keygen,
  encrypt, decrypt, base64url helpers.
- `frontend/src/App.vue`: Top-level Vue 3 component, routes by URL path.
- `frontend/src/components/Compose.vue`: drag-drop encrypt + upload.
- `frontend/src/components/Viewer.vue`: peek + confirm + decrypt + save.
- `tests/`: pytest suite — server validation, one-time-download,
  capability-URL authorization invariants.

## How to run

    python3 -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
    pip install -r requirements.txt
    (cd frontend && npm install && npm run build)
    uvicorn app.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000
